rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE AUDREY G. WILLIAMS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Approval Of Legal Fees And Out Of Pocket Costs To Former Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. (1) The court may need to review the trustee's future account (Sixth and Final Account covering the account period from 7/1/22 once filed before ruling on this request. CRC 7.752. (2) Need clarification on whether or not the services provided listed in the attached billing statements overlap with those listed in the billing statement attached to the Conservator's Sixth and Final Account filed on 3/28/2024. This petition page 3, starting line 23 states the legal service provided to the conservatorship while the fee declaration attached to the conservatorship also asked for the fees during the same period. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHERINE L. FREEMAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/13/24. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The proof of service e-filed on 6/12/24 shows notice of the petition, not the amended petition. Notice of the 8/13/24 hearing of the amended petition must be served at least 15 days before that hearing. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address this issue must be received by the assigned examiner by 8/2/24. If, however, a petition for final distribution is filed in the meantime (and set for a future hearing by the clerk), this petition could just be taken off calendar as moot. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (John Cuerva was appointed as the special administrator on 10/14/22; he was never appointed as a personal representative. A pleading proof of service attached to a Probate petition is meaningless; the only proper form of notice for Probate petitions is a Judicial Council form Notice of Hearing, on the reverse side of which is the proof of service that should be completed. If a pleading form of proof of service is filed, a copy of the Notice of Hearing that was served must be attached to it to show that the correct notice was served. Kathy Nakagawaâs bond was $350,000.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNY LEA BROWNSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/13/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service has been filed. (3) Because the conservatee is now reported to have died, the conservator might wish to amend this second account to be a second and final account, extending the ending date of the accounting period to 5/22/24 and incorporating the additional accounting for the period following the date of death. [Probate Code Section 2620(b).] (4) The amended first account showed an ending balance as of 1/31/22. This accounting begins on 2/19/22 (not 6/22/22, as claimed in paragraph 3 of the petition), so that the starting value of this accounting does not agree with the ending value from the amended first account. (5) Who is Jeanine Jue, and why were there refunds received from her? (6) Whether or not the Judicial Council forms are used by a standard account filer, a subsequent account must state the date of any order authorizing payment of fiduciary or attorney fees. [CRC 7.575(e)(6)(C).] See Judicial Council forms GC-400(PH)(1) and GC-400(C)(4). (7) The 1/4/23 order settling the amended first account ordered the conservator to file a Supplemental I&A to report the conservatee's Lincoln National life insurance policy and New York Life annuities. No such I&A appears to have been filed. (8) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (9) Attorney costs without adequate descriptions (e.g., "One Hour Delivery Service" without more) is not sufficient. (10) Why was there an e-filing service charge of $5.00 for the amended first accounting? The amended first account was not e-filed. (11) There is no authority for a Court-appointed fiduciary to pay legal fees out of his own assets or for the attorney to receive such payment without a Court order and then to seek reimbursement. [CRC 7.755(a).] Deadline is 8/2/24 for providing the assigned examiner a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The fee declaration is rather confusing, broken up into multiple time periods. The time sheets should be for the entire period for which services were provided and for which payment is sought.)

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/02/2024, as the following issues remain unaddressed: 1) No DE-147S form is on file. 2) Re the DE-121 form: i) Tyron Nunley's address is different from his address the Court has on file (correctional facility in St. Louis, MI). See also CRC 7.51(a)(2) ("A notice mailed to a person in care of another person is insufficient unless the person entitled to notice is an adult and has directed the party giving notice in writing to send the notice in care of the second person."). ii) The address of Terrence Capers is different from the address listed on Attachment 8 (416 Downey Ave. Apt. 5A, Modesto, CA). iii) Sheila Foster (minor Ean Foster's custodian) is not listed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VICTORIA C. DRAYTON
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Entitlement To Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court is to determine whether to take this petition under submission absent any further objection, by accepting the response and supplement included in the petitioner's status report filed on 04/29/2024 (and set for 07/02/2024) as sufficient to address the issues previously raised by the Court, even though it was not filed as a separate document captioned as a supplement to this petition. A review of the proposed order indicates that the following modifications are necessary: 1) The share of Douglas Lucas, Isa Lucas, and Dawon Lucas should be 1/1400 each (and not 1/840 as so stated in the proposed order). 2) The share of Dontrey Cote and Carl Lucas should be 1/1400 each (and not 1/960 as so stated in the proposed order). rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES DEA
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. The "supplement" e-filed on 6/4/24, however, amended the petition (to correctly reduce the statutory fee), and notice was mailed before the "supplement" was e-filed on 6/4/24, contrary to LRSF 14.14 A. Attorney costs were also not detailed (date, payee, description, and amount for each separate cost). No SASE was provided, so no copy of the order will be returned.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. WELLMAN 2004 TRUST, DATED APRIL 27, 2004 AS AMENDED AND RESTATED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For: (1) Court Order Finding That The 2004 Trust Was Amended By The 2017 Agreement To Remove Sunny V.H. Luong And His Family Members As
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 08/21/2024 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 06/11/2024). The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 3) If the settlor's surviving spouse is not a beneficiary of the trust, but may qualify as an omitted spouse within the meaning of section 21610, the surviving spouse must be served in the manner required by section 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. 4) No trustee notification under section 16061.7 was attached to Exhibit 8. Please provide a copy of the notification. 5) Sunny Luong's street address must be provided by a verified allegation, and direct notice must be given, absent a signed waiver of direct notice. CRC 7.51(a)(2) & section 1204. 6) If Sunny Luong's residence or usual place of business is not located in San Francisco County, please provide specific examples of the day-to-day activity of the trust carried on by Sunny Luong as trustee within San Francisco County. See section 17002. 7) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to describe efforts to locate Mary Luu and Douglass Huynh. Whehter notice can be prescribed in any manner other than mailing (e.g., telephone, e-mail, social media, etc.) must also be addressed. See CRC 7.52(c), section 1212, and CCP section 413.30. 8) Each charitable beneficiary designated by the trustee pursuant to section 5.3(c) of the trust must be identified by a verified allegation and receive notice. If no charity has been designated, notice must be given to the Attorney General at its office in Sacramento. See sections 1209 & 17203(a)(3). 9) If the settlor and Sunny Luong registered their domestic partnership with the Secretary of State, please provide a copy of a judgment that dissolved their domestic partnership. 10) Please provide a copy of the settlor's will.
